subject: Re: Fwd: Epstein
body:
In the new brief,, we need legislative history,trafficking,  intent to target minors,, case law on scienter, precedent, we should deal with the large numbers, no direct contact, in many instances the girls called us.. , purpose for travel..etc.. Also please note.. that this should be sent to littlestjeff@yahoo.com

----- Original Message ----
From: Mitch Webber <mwebber@law.harvard.edu>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Cc: dersh@law.harvard.edu; avid@mycingular.blackberry.net
Sent: Sunday, October 22, 2006 10:43:09 AM
Subject: Re: Fwd: Epstein





I can't open it either.  I don't know why they use
WordPerfect.  I'm out of town right now, but we'll send it from
another format from the office first thing tomorrow.


- Mitch 


At 07:38 AM 10/22/2006 -0700, you wrote:

I can't open the wordpad file..
could you send a differnt format

From: Mitch Webber <mwebber@law.harvard.edu>

Sent: Saturday, October 21, 2006 12:21:58 PM

Subject: Fwd: Epstein




Mr. Epstein,


Here's the memo that I worked on with Vicky Eiger.


In case you didn't get the message yesterday, we're trying to figure out


how the Riley confusion was cleared up with Mr. Krischer.  We think
Roy 

Black sent an e-mail explaining everything.


Best,

Mitch

ndershowitz@lawdea.com
To: dersh@law.harvard.edu

We can make the point we discussed on the phone by
substituting this
paragraph for the final paragraph of the memo that
I sent you.

           Thus, if the defendant pleads

guilty to aggravated

assault, there is a grave risk that under existing
state and Virgin
Islands statutes, he will be subject to sex
offender registration
requirments and/or criminal exposure for failure
to register. There is a
graver risk still that there will be future
expansion of the statutes or
expansive construction of them that will mean he
is covered or arguably
so. Many people who pled guilty to offenses when
no registration
requirements existed have been surprised to find
themselves subjected to
registration laws enacted or expanded after their
guilty pleas were
entered. The defendant should not have to live
with that Sword of
Damocles hanging over his head.
